``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Generally, prison sentences include a judicial maximum term but no minimum term. Most people in prison are eligible for discretionary parole release immediately upon admission.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were people in prison past their parole consideration year. This group made up of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::

## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 9%, changing from 9,455 in 2010 to 8,562 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics The demographics of people in prison are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and Black, non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity
## Criminal Offenses In 2020, 48% of people in prison were incarcerated for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for property (19%) and other or unknown (17%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison had original sentence lengths between 10 to 24.9 years representing 36%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases increased 13%, dropping from 4,571 in 2010 to 5,181 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Iowa. The largest change was for rape or sexual assault offenses, which increased by 70%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Iowa, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 9.6 times higher and Hispanic, any race people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 1.7 times higher and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 1.5 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Black people faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 1.9 years. Other races people experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 1.3 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For rape or sexual assault offenses, Black people had 7.1 more years on average compared to people of Other* races, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
